records.question: In Formula 1 seasons since 2001, considering only drivers who scored points in a season, which five constructors have had the most seasons where their drivers scored the fewest total points among all point-scoring drivers in that season?
records.pred_sql:
WITH driver_season_points AS (
    SELECT 
        res.driver_id,
        r.year,
        SUM(res.points) AS total_points
    FROM results res
    JOIN races r ON res.race_id = r.race_id
    WHERE r.year >= 2001
    GROUP BY res.driver_id, r.year
    HAVING SUM(res.points) > 0
),
constructor_season_points AS (
    SELECT 
        res.constructor_id,
        r.year,
        SUM(res.points) AS total_points
    FROM results res
    JOIN races r ON res.race_id = r.race_id
    JOIN driver_season_points dsp ON res.driver_id = dsp.driver_id AND r.year = dsp.year
    WHERE r.year >= 2001
    GROUP BY res.constructor_id, r.year
),
min_points_per_season AS (
    SELECT 
        year,
        MIN(total_points) AS min_points
    FROM constructor_season_points
    GROUP BY year
),
constructor_min_seasons AS (
    SELECT 
        csp.constructor_id,
        COUNT(*) AS seasons_count
    FROM constructor_season_points csp
    JOIN min_points_per_season mps ON csp.year = mps.year AND csp.total_points = mps.min_points
    GROUP BY csp.constructor_id
)
SELECT 
    c.name,
    cm.seasons_count
FROM constructor_min_seasons cm
JOIN constructors c ON cm.constructor_id = c.constructor_id
ORDER BY cm.seasons_count DESC
LIMIT 5

records.question: In the first five laps of the race, how many overtakes occurred in each category—retirements, pit stops, start-related overtakes, and standard on-track passes?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM retirements r 
            WHERE r.race_id = lp1.race_id AND r.lap = lp1.lap AND r.driver_id <> lp1.driver_id
        ) THEN 'retirements'
        WHEN lp1.lap_type = 'Retirement (Disqualification)' THEN 'retirements'
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM pit_stops ps 
            WHERE ps.race_id = lp1.race_id AND ps.driver_id = lp1.driver_id AND ps.lap = lp1.lap
        ) THEN 'pit stops'
        WHEN lp1.lap = 1 THEN 'start-related'
        ELSE 'standard on-track passes'
    END AS category,
    SUM(lp2.position - lp1.position) AS overtake_count
FROM lap_positions lp1
JOIN lap_positions lp2 
  ON lp1.race_id = lp2.race_id 
 AND lp1.driver_id = lp2.driver_id 
 AND lp1.lap = lp2.lap + 1
WHERE lp1.lap BETWEEN 1 AND 5
  AND lp1.position < lp2.position
GROUP BY category

records.question: Considering all races where pit stop data is available, and focusing on instances when a driver was not behind another car on the previous lap but is behind on the current lap (accounting for retirements, pit-stop entries, pit-stop exits, and race starts), how many times has each type of overtake occurred in Formula 1?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN prev_lap.lap_type = 'N' AND cur_lap.lap_type = 'N' THEN 'On Track'
    WHEN prev_lap.lap_type = 'P' AND cur_lap.lap_type = 'N' THEN 'Pit Stop Overcut'
    WHEN prev_lap.lap_type = 'N' AND cur_lap.lap_type = 'P' THEN 'Pit Stop Undercut'
    WHEN cur_lap.lap_type = 'R' THEN 'Retirement'
    ELSE 'Other'
  END AS overtake_type,
  COUNT(*) AS overtake_count
FROM lap_positions cur_lap
JOIN lap_positions prev_lap 
  ON cur_lap.race_id = prev_lap.race_id 
 AND cur_lap.driver_id = prev_lap.driver_id 
 AND cur_lap.lap = prev_lap.lap + 1
JOIN races ON cur_lap.race_id = races.race_id
JOIN races_ext ON races.race_id = races_ext.race_id 
 AND races_ext.is_pit_data_available = 1
WHERE cur_lap.position < prev_lap.position
  AND prev_lap.lap IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY overtake_type

records.question: Among Formula 1 drivers who raced during the 1950s, which drivers completed a season in that decade with the same constructor in both the first and the last race they participated in, while also taking part in at least two distinct race rounds during that season?
records.pred_sql:
WITH driver_seasons AS (
    SELECT 
        d.driver_id, 
        r.year,
        MIN(r.round) AS first_round,
        MAX(r.round) AS last_round,
        COUNT(DISTINCT r.round) AS num_rounds
    FROM drivers d
    JOIN results res ON d.driver_id = res.driver_id
    JOIN races r ON res.race_id = r.race_id
    WHERE r.year BETWEEN 1950 AND 1959
    GROUP BY d.driver_id, r.year
    HAVING num_rounds >= 2
),
first_last_constructors AS (
    SELECT 
        ds.driver_id, 
        ds.year,
        fc.constructor_id AS first_constructor_id,
        lc.constructor_id AS last_constructor_id
    FROM driver_seasons ds
    JOIN results fc ON ds.driver_id = fc.driver_id
    JOIN races fr ON fc.race_id = fr.race_id 
        AND fr.year = ds.year 
        AND fr.round = ds.first_round
    JOIN results lc ON ds.driver_id = lc.driver_id
    JOIN races lr ON lc.race_id = lr.race_id 
        AND lr.year = ds.year 
        AND lr.round = ds.last_round
)
SELECT DISTINCT 
    d.driver_id,

records.question: Calculate the overall average first round and average last round of races missed by Formula 1 drivers across all years. Include only drivers who missed fewer than three races in a given year and who switched teams between their appearances immediately before and after their hiatus (i.e., the constructor ID for the race right before their first missed race must be different from the constructor ID for the race right after their last missed race in that year). Do not group results by year; return just the overall averages across the entire dataset.
records.pred_sql:
WITH ordered_drives AS (
    SELECT year,
           driver_id,
           constructor_id,
           first_round,
           last_round,
           LAG(last_round) OVER (
               PARTITION BY year, driver_id 
               ORDER BY first_round
           ) AS prev_last_round,
           LAG(constructor_id) OVER (
               PARTITION BY year, driver_id 
               ORDER BY first_round
           ) AS prev_constructor
    FROM drives
)
SELECT AVG(prev_last_round + 1) AS avg_first_round,
       AVG(first_round - 1) AS avg_last_round
FROM ordered_drives
WHERE prev_last_round IS NOT NULL
  AND (first_round - prev_last_round - 1) BETWEEN 1 AND 2
  AND constructor_id != prev_constructor

records.question: Provide the full names of drivers who have been overtaken on track more times than they have overtaken others on track during race laps, excluding position changes due to pit stops (both at pit entry and exit), retirements, or position changes that occurred during the first lap of a race (considered as start movements).
records.pred_sql:
WITH driver_deltas AS (
  SELECT 
    curr.race_id,
    curr.driver_id,
    (prev.position - curr.position) AS delta
  FROM lap_positions curr
  JOIN lap_positions prev 
    ON curr.race_id = prev.race_id 
   AND curr.driver_id = prev.driver_id 
   AND curr.lap = prev.lap + 1
  WHERE curr.lap > 1
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM pit_stops p1 
      WHERE p1.race_id = curr.race_id 
        AND p1.driver_id = curr.driver_id 
        AND p1.lap = curr.lap
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM pit_stops p2 
      WHERE p2.race_id = curr.race_id 
        AND p2.driver_id = curr.driver_id 
        AND p2.lap = prev.lap
    )
)
SELECT d.full_name
FROM driver_deltas dd
JOIN drivers d ON dd.driver_id = d.driver_id
GROUP BY d.driver_id, d.full_name
HAVING SUM(dd.delta) < 0

records.question: How many users are there in each age category (20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, and others)?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN (strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', birth_date) - (strftime('%m-%d', 'now') < strftime('%m-%d', birth_date))) BETWEEN 20 AND 29 THEN '20s'
    WHEN (strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', birth_date) - (strftime('%m-%d', 'now') < strftime('%m-%d', birth_date))) BETWEEN 30 AND 39 THEN '30s'
    WHEN (strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', birth_date) - (strftime('%m-%d', 'now') < strftime('%m-%d', birth_date))) BETWEEN 40 AND 49 THEN '40s'
    WHEN (strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', birth_date) - (strftime('%m-%d', 'now') < strftime('%m-%d', birth_date))) BETWEEN 50 AND 59 THEN '50s'
    ELSE 'others'
  END AS age_category,
  COUNT(*) AS user_count
FROM mst_users
GROUP BY age_category

records.question: For each user session in the activity log table, identify the number of events that occurred before the first '/detail' click or '/complete' conversion, counting only events that have a non-empty search type. Find the sessions with the minimum count of such pre-click/pre-conversion events. If multiple sessions share this minimum count, include all of them in the results. Return each qualifying session along with the corresponding path and search type.
records.pred_sql:
WITH first_target AS (
  SELECT session, stamp, path, search_type
  FROM (
    SELECT session, stamp, path, search_type,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp) AS rn
    FROM activity_log
    WHERE path IN ('/detail', '/complete')
  ) WHERE rn = 1
),
pre_counts AS (
  SELECT a.session, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM activity_log a
  JOIN first_target t ON a.session = t.session
  WHERE a.stamp < t.stamp
    AND a.search_type IS NOT NULL
    AND a.search_type != ''
  GROUP BY a.session
),
min_cnt AS (
  SELECT MIN(cnt) AS mincnt FROM pre_counts
)
SELECT p.session, t.path, t.search_type
FROM pre_counts p
JOIN first_target t ON p.session = t.session
CROSS JOIN min_cnt m
WHERE p.cnt = m.mincnt
ORDER BY p.session
